extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo won the 31.9km time trial by 2 seconds over VAN AERT Wout, who led by 11 seconds at first split but lost 22 seconds in the middle sector.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT Wout lost 22 seconds between first and second intermediate splits despite dominating opening technical section, revealing pacing vulnerability.
GANNA Filippo's 39-second advantage at second split was nearly erased, losing 9 seconds in final kilometer despite slight uphill favoring power.
CATTANEO Mattia placed third overall in TT, putting three Jumbo-Visma riders in top four GC, suggesting deliberate team leader confusion tactics.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: dominant technical opening (11s up) but catastrophic middle sector (22s lost) indicates pacing misjudgment or diesel engine characteristics
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: 53.9 km/h average on 31.9km course with technical start shows he can win even when losing time in closing kilometer
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: positive: second fastest at intermediate split and third overall while wearing national champion skinsuit instead of green suggests form peak
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: fifth place TT result while 56 seconds down on GC suggests Jumbo prioritizing Vingegaard as protected leader
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: seventh in TT while starting only 16 seconds down GC is weak signal for Tour de France TT capabilities
rider_signals.CATTANEO Mattia: positive: fourth in TT and second on GC at 53 seconds demonstrates Quick-Step has unexpected GC threat beyond support role
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: positive: fifth fastest at second intermediate split shows he can still produce competitive TT efforts in stage race context
rider_signals.DURBRIDGE Luke: positive: went early, set standard with 53s deficit to Ganna, held top six despite starting over hour before favorites
